nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef slopes, lagoons, and turbid inshore zones; typically found on rock, rubble, and dead coral substrate in lower-light, lower-flow positions

decorPreferences: live rock or rubble base for attachment
lower to mid rockwork placement in moderate-to-bright lit tanks
spacing from neighboring corals to prevent chemical aggression

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via symbiotic zooxanthellae. Benefits from supplemental target-feeding of small meaty foods such as brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, or micro-plankton approximately once per week per polyp. Amino acid supplements have been reported to improve growth rate and disc coloration. Generally tolerates slightly nutrient-rich water better than SPS or LPS corals.

goodWith: other Discosoma mushroom corals with adequate spacing
avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals in direct contact (torch, hammer, frogspawn, chalice)
large urchins that may dislodge specimens
strong-flow powerheads aimed directly at polyps

summary: Spotted Mushroom Coral (Discosoma sp.) is a beginner-friendly corallimorpharian distinguished by raised spots and striated disc patterns in contrasting hues. Hardy and fast-reproducing; thrives under low-moderate light and gentle flow.
careNotes:
Taxonomic and synonymy note: This entry covers Discosoma sp. as sold under the "Spotted Mushroom Coral" and "Spotted Actinodiscus Mushroom Coral" trade names. Discosoma Rüppell & Leuckart, 1828 is the currently accepted genus name; Actinodiscus de Blainville, 1830 is a widely cited junior synonym. Despite the scientific synonymy, many retailers (including LiveAquaria) continue to list products under the Actinodiscus trade name. See also the sibling entry [actinodiscus-coral] for the general Actinodiscus / Discosoma plain-morph entry. This Discosoma entry focuses on the spotted and striated trade variants, which are characterized by decorative raised spots with varying shades of contrasting coloration across the disc surface.

Lighting: Generally advised to keep at low-to-moderate intensity, approximately 40–100 PAR for stable captive keeping. ReefChasers suggests 50–100 PAR as the recommended sweet spot for Discosoma; values up to 150 PAR have been reported as tolerated. Bleaching under high-intensity lighting is commonly reported — start specimens in a lower tank position and acclimate upward slowly over several weeks.

Flow: Low to moderate flow is preferred. Direct powerhead current has been known to cause shrinking, tissue stress, and detachment from the substrate. Indirect or alternating gentle flow is generally advisable; polyps should sit open and relaxed, not compressed or folded. The LiveAquaria "Mushroom Coral, Spotted" listing specifies "Medium" flow, while the ReefChasers guide recommends low-to-moderate; intermediate flow is appropriate.

Placement: Flexible positioning is generally acceptable (LiveAquaria lists "All"), though lower to mid placements are commonly advised for tanks with higher-intensity lighting rigs, since this naturally reduces PAR at the coral's position.

Aggression: Semi-aggressive — like all corallimorpharians, Discosoma has been known to sting neighboring corals via direct contact and allelopathic chemical release when colonizing in numbers. Adequate spacing from other coral types is generally advised. Running activated carbon is sometimes recommended in tanks with dense mushroom coverage to mitigate allelopathy.

Temperature source spread: LiveAquaria lists 72–78°F (22–26°C); ReefChasers lists 77–84°F (25–29°C). These ranges partially overlap but diverge at both ends. An averaged conservative range of 22–27°C is used; values up to 28°C may be tolerated short-term but are not well-supported as target values across both sources.

Nutrient tolerance: Unlike SPS corals, Discosoma generally tolerates slightly elevated nitrate (5–10 ppm) and phosphate (0.05–0.1 ppm). Ultra-low nutrient systems may cause browning out of spot coloration. Moderate nutrients can support more vivid spotted patterning.

Spotted morph distinction: The spotted trade variant is characterized by raised tubercles (bumps) on the disc surface arranged in spotted or striated patterns, often in contrasting hues (green on brown, purple on blue, orange on tan, etc.). This is a consistent visual trade distinction from plain Discosoma / Actinodiscus morphs. Care requirements are identical between spotted and plain morphs.

Reproduction: Reproduces readily in captivity via longitudinal fission, pedal laceration, and occasional budding. Under stable conditions, colonies have been reported to multiply rapidly and colonize available substrate. Fragging by cutting mature polyps is a commonly practiced propagation method.

Handling: All corallimorpharians possess nematocysts. Gloves are generally recommended when handling; hands should be washed thoroughly after contact.
breedingNotes: Reproduces asexually in captivity via longitudinal fission, pedal laceration, and occasional budding. Under stable conditions, specimens have been reported to multiply rapidly and colonize available substrate. Fragging by cutting mature polyps is a commonly practiced propagation method in the hobby. Sexual reproduction occurs on natural reefs but is not practiced in captivity.

primary.name: LiveAquaria – Mushroom Coral, Spotted
primary.notes: Primary species page for the Spotted Mushroom Coral trade variant (Actinodiscus sp. / Discosoma sp.). Also consulted the Spotted Actinodiscus Mushroom Coral 3.75" WYSIWYG page which gave identical parameters. Confirmed: temperature 72–78°F, SG 1.023–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, lighting Moderate, flow Medium, placement All, temperament Semi-Aggressive, care level Easy. Key morphological note: 'decorative raised spots with varying shades of contrasting coloration.' Adult size range listed as 1–5 inches (2.5–12.7 cm) across small/medium/large grades.

additional.name: ReefChasers – Discosoma Mushroom Coral Care Guide
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Confirmed beginner care level and Indo-Pacific origin. PAR 50–100 recommended (75–150 tolerated), flow low-to-moderate, pH 8.0–8.4, dKH 8–9, calcium 400–450 ppm, temperature 77–84°F (25–29°C). Temperature upper bound (84°F/28.9°C) is higher than LiveAquaria's 78°F ceiling; averaged conservative range of 22–27°C used. ReefChasers confirms Actinodiscus spp. = Discosoma synonymy. Magnesium not specified; values from sibling actinodiscus-coral entry used (1200–1350 ppm).

additional.name: Wikipedia – Discosoma
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy only. Confirmed: Family Discosomidae, Order Corallimorpharia, approximately 11 recognized species. Actinodiscus de Blainville 1830 confirmed as accepted synonym of Discosoma Rüppell & Leuckart 1828. Physical description confirms disc-shaped form with spotted and striated color patterns. Not used for husbandry parameters.

additional.name: LiveAquaria – Spotted Actinodiscus Mushroom Coral (WYSIWYG listing)
additional.notes: Supplementary LiveAquaria listing confirming identical parameters: temperature 72–78°F, SG 1.023–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, lighting Moderate, flow Low-to-Medium, placement Bottom-to-Middle, care Easy, temperament Semi-Aggressive. Minor flow/placement discrepancy between this listing (Low-to-Medium / Bottom-to-Middle) and the Mushroom Coral Spotted listing (Medium / All); intermediate values used in JSON.
